tldr: FingerprintJS to Kount moves you from a focused device identification signal to a full fraud platform with AI scoring and a Mastercard-backed merchant network. You will trade simple signal calls for a deeper integration that issues recommended actions.
why_switch: Kount provides scoring and recommended actions, not just signals
Backed by Mastercard's merchant network for shared fraud intelligence
Better fit for payment and CNP fraud workflows
Free tier available for getting started; consult Kount's official pricing page for current rates
Mature case management and rules tooling out of the box
what_to_export: Smart Signals usage and event payloads
export_method: FingerprintJS exposes identification results and Smart Signals through its Server API and webhooks. To export, pull historical identification events from the Server API for the date ranges you operate on, and rely on your own application logs for the user-to-visitor-id mappings since you typically own the richest copy. Save webhook URLs, signing keys, and any per-route or per-event configuration you have. Document where the JS agent and mobile SDKs are installed (which routes, which event types) so the new platform can be instrumented in the same places. Capture API keys and any secret references in your code. Run the export alongside continued production traffic, verify counts, and only then disconnect FingerprintJS.
import_method: Kount typically integrates with a client-side data collector on checkout, login, and signup pages and a server-side API call that returns a risk score and recommended action. Provision a Kount account, generate API credentials, install the data collector on the same pages where the Fingerprint agent ran, and instrument backend services to send transaction or event payloads. Replace your code paths that read FingerprintJS Smart Signals with calls to Kount's scoring API. If you previously fed Fingerprint signals into your own rules engine, replace those rules with Kount rules or keep them and feed Kount's score in as a strong feature. Configure webhooks for asynchronous verdicts. Run Kount in shadow mode alongside FingerprintJS on real traffic, compare outcomes against historical fraud labels, tune thresholds, then cut over.

concept_mapping.target: Customer or session identifier in Kount

gotchas: Kount offers verdicts; if you only had Fingerprint signals + custom rules, you must redesign decisioning
Lock-in increases vs FingerprintJS; integration is deeper
Pricing structure is not fully transparent — request a quote and verify in current docs before committing
Bot mitigation coverage differs; validate during shadow mode
Visitor identifier reputation does not transfer; backfill where possible
Privacy and data flow review may be needed for the additional payload sent to Kount

email.subject: Moving fraud detection from FingerprintJS to Kount
email.body:
Hi team,

We are migrating our fraud detection from FingerprintJS to Kount over the next 14 days. Kount gives us scoring and recommended actions on top of device data, plus access to a Mastercard-backed merchant network.

What to expect:
- The Kount data collector will be installed on checkout, login, and signup pages, replacing the Fingerprint agent there.
- Backend services will call Kount for scoring rather than reading Smart Signals.
- Custom rules that consumed Fingerprint signals will be reimplemented as Kount rules or kept with Kount's score as input.
- We will run both systems in parallel for the first week and compare outcomes before cutover.

Please flag any service that depends on FingerprintJS lookups so we can route it to the new pipeline. Reach out with questions before the end of the week.

Thanks,
Your customer
